required_generation_path_contract: raw model-native metrics must be read separately from constrained, repaired, inferred-bond, reranked, or backend-scored metrics

axes.interprets: Whether reported quality depends on target-ligand denoising semantics versus an explicit ligand-refinement mode that still does not support de novo wording.

Whether model behavior still produces bounded pocket-conditioned rollouts when target ligand atom types and coordinates are not used for decoder initialization; this uses the shape-safe surrogate objective, remains a baseline, and does not support de novo wording.
Whether full molecular flow can generate from a pocket-conditioned scaffold without target-ligand topology or geometry initialization.
Whether graph message passing improves quality, geometry, efficiency, slot usage, gate usage, and leakage relative to the lightweight atom-degree baseline.
Whether bond-type-aware message passing improves chemically meaningful topology representations relative to untyped adjacency message passing.
Whether translation-invariant pair-distance kernels improve geometry consistency and pocket fit over raw coordinate projection.
Whether ligand-local-frame pair messages improve geometry consistency and pocket fit over distance-only radial kernels.
Whether local pocket message passing improves contact, clash, and context-specialization behavior over independent feature projection.
Whether ligand-relative local-frame pocket messages improve pocket-context specialization over radius-bounded local message passing.
Whether slot decomposition contributes beyond direct modality representations.
Whether quality, leakage, and slot stability are sensitive to representation capacity.
Whether masking inactive slots out of cross-attention and decoder-local attention is necessary for stable utilization and leakage control.
Whether leakage diagnostics and penalties affect specialization without overstating absence of off-modality information.
Whether the differentiable leakage penalty contributes to specialization beyond passive leakage diagnostics.
Whether intra-modality redundancy reduction contributes to quality, slot stability, leakage, and efficiency.
Negative-control comparison that keeps only topology slots visible downstream while preserving separate encoders.
Negative-control comparison that keeps only geometry slots visible downstream while preserving separate encoders.
Negative-control comparison that keeps only pocket/context slots visible downstream while preserving separate encoders.
Whether semantic probes are carrying useful specialization signal or only reporting passive diagnostics.
Whether sparse controlled interaction gates improve quality or only suppress needed cross-modality dependence.
Whether changing the sigmoid gate temperature alters controlled cross-modality usage independently of the gate sparsity loss weight.
Negative-control comparison that forces directed paths open and must not be presented as the preferred controlled-interaction architecture.
Whether local atom-slot and pocket conditioning improves generation quality over global mean-pooled summaries.
How much reported quality depends on repair rather than raw model-native generation.
Whether staged warm-starting is necessary compared with activating configured objective families from step zero.
